caption.text:
Heartbreak sucks. And here’s the hard truth: the only way to get over someone is to actually go through it 💔

There’s no shortcut, no hack, no “revenge glow-up” that skips the pain. When someone cheats, leaves, or breaks up with you, they’ve just shown you exactly who they are.

You don’t heal by holding on. You heal by letting go.

Listen to this full conversation with @jayshetty on the @jayshettypodcast 🎧 "Mel Robbins: How to Use the ‘Let Them Theory’ (A Life Changing Mindset Hack That 15 Million People Can’t Stop Talking About)"

He lost everything in 90 seconds… and found peace in just 7.

Dave made one risky move during a football game and watched his daughter’s entire college fund disappear.

The guilt was unbearable.
He couldn’t sleep. He couldn’t breathe. He felt like he’d destroyed everything he worked for.

And for weeks… he stayed stuck in that regret.

Until one night, while searching for peace, Dave came across a strange 7-second sound ritual quietly circulating through the manifestation world.

💡 Known as the “7-Second Abundance Shift,” this audio ritual blends ancient Eastern sound healing with modern brainwave science—designed to clear emotional clutter and help you tune into a calmer, clearer, more aligned version of yourself.

No meditating for hours. No visual boards. Just 7 seconds… and a pair of headphones.

Dave tried it.
Then again.
And then something inside started to shift…

Clarity replaced chaos.
Peace replaced panic.
And from that space—he rebuilt. Slowly, powerfully, and with purpose.

He’s not alone.
💬 Thousands of people are now weaving this 7-second sound into their daily ritual—and reporting real emotional breakthroughs. (Individual results will always vary.)

caption.text:
1. Reframe, don’t erase.
You can’t delete someone from your mind, but you can change how you see them.
Love often grows from idealization — we see what we want to see.
Start by remembering the moments that hurt: when they ignored you, dismissed you, or made you feel small.
This helps your brain shift from attachment to awareness.

2. Replace fantasy with facts.
Write down how this person made you feel on your worst days.
Be brutally honest — no filters, no excuses.
When you read it back slowly, your mind begins to separate emotional longing from the truth.
That’s where detachment starts.

3. Visualize release, not revenge.
Close your eyes and picture them walking away.
But this time, don’t chase.
Imagine turning around and walking in the opposite direction.
Repeat this often — your mind learns through repetition, and each time, the emotional hook weakens.

4. Redirect the dopamine loop.
When you feel the urge to check their profile, text them, or replay a memory — pause.
Do one small act of self-focus instead: take a walk, write in your journal, breathe deeply.
Over time, the same dopamine that once bonded you to them begins bonding you back to yourself.

5. Understand the science behind it.
Studies show that when people consciously practice reframing and visualization, brain scans reveal reduced activity in the area linked to romantic obsession.
You don’t forget love — you just stop confusing pain for love.

6. Train your mind, not just your heart.
Don’t wait for time to heal you.
Healing happens when you train your mind to let go — and your heart naturally follows.

💫 This is exactly what I teach you in my program!
Transform your mind, energy, and life in just 10 sessions. DM to connect with me 1:1

caption.text:
@Moviezar posts the best movie and series content on Instagram daily 🎬

Meet Joe Black (November 1998) is a romantic fantasy drama directed by Martin Brest, starring Brad Pitt, Anthony Hopkins, and Claire Forlani. The film follows media mogul William Parrish (Hopkins) whose life takes a surreal turn when Death, personified as Joe Black (Pitt), arrives to guide him into the afterlife. As Joe experiences human life and love, he forms a deep connection with Parrish’s daughter, Susan (Forlani), challenging the natural order of life and mortality.

The movie explores themes of love, loss, and the fleeting nature of existence, blending heartfelt emotion with philosophical reflection.

👉 Fun fact: The film’s runtime is nearly three hours, and Brad Pitt reportedly prepared extensively to capture the calm, enigmatic presence of Death in a human form.

caption.text:
1. Ask her what parts of herself she sees in you — she’s noticed more than she’s ever said.

2. Go through her old jewelry box and let her tell you which pieces she wore during the biggest moments of her life.

3. Ask her what she wishes someone had told her about being a woman when she was your age.

4. Stand with her in the mirror and ask what she hopes you understand about real beauty as you get older.

5. Cook a recipe she learned from her mom and ask what memories come back for her.

6. Ask her about the moment she felt closest to you when you were a kid — that story will stick with you.

7. Have her teach you something she does without even thinking — the way she folds clothes, seasons food, or comforts people.

8. Look at photos of her before she became a mom and ask what dreams she had back then.

9. Ask what part of being a mom surprised her the most — good or tough.

10. Spend a morning doing her routine with her — coffee, skincare, her little habits.

11. Ask what lesson she hopes you take into your future relationships or marriage.

12. Take a quiet walk together and let her talk about the things she never brings up.

13. Ask her when she realized you weren’t a little kid anymore — and how that felt.

14. Tell her one thing she did that made you feel safe growing up.

15. Ask what she hopes you remember about her when you’re her age someday.

——

One day you’re gonna look in the mirror and see a little bit of her looking back at you — and you’ll wish you asked her more while you still had the chance.

caption.text:
1.	In 1965, Rosenthal ran an experiment that many later tried to discredit because the results were inconvenient. Participants were told that certain people in the study were “more gifted,” even though they had been chosen at random. Within eight weeks, those “gifted” participants started acting as if they genuinely were superior, they made decisions faster, doubted themselves less, and their performance increased by almost a quarter.

	2.	Years later, researchers in Zurich repeated the experiment on adults. One group was told they would “perform better” because they supposedly had a “hidden cognitive resource.” The tests were identical to the other group’s, but within 40 minutes their brains began producing answers 89% faster. The researchers said: “We didn’t increase their abilities, we changed their expectations of themselves.”

	3.	One participant admitted he felt a clarity he hadn’t experienced in years: “I just thought I could, and then I could.” Rosenthal insisted there was nothing mystical about it: people act not from their actual abilities, but from what they believe is possible for them. Give someone a version of the future that feels real, and the body begins to behave as if it has already happened.

	4.	The darkest conclusion from the experiment: the Rosenthal effect works in reverse too. If someone is made to believe they are “difficult,” “faulty,” or “a failure,” the brain starts confirming that script, as if it were testing a hypothesis planted in childhood. Neuroscientists have shown that negative expectations from others slow neural transmission almost as much as chronic fatigue.

	5.	At a Harvard conference, a psychologist said a line that made the room go silent:
“We become who others think we are, unless we realize in time that this voice isn’t ours.”
He added that when a person changes not their motivation, but their forecast of themselves, the psyche reorganizes instantly, as if it receives a new operating instruction.

Have you noticed that the biggest turning points in life happen right after you stop believing someone else’s prediction of who you are?

We may never cross paths again. Follow so you don’t lose me - @arturrioni

caption.text:
"If we never meet again…
I want you to know that love doesn’t truly disappear.
It moves quietly into the world, finding its way back in moments, in people, in memories we can’t always see. 💙

Who do you feel this for? Tag them below or just let your heart sit with these words.

If words like this speak to you, my poetry eBook Letters to the Heavy-Hearted is full of reflections for anyone loving, missing, or healing.

Find it through the link in bio 💙

#LovePoems #HealingWords #LettersToTheHeavyHearted. 

Clip by @puuung love the work

caption.text:
Trusting the Timing of Your Journey.

Lucy Hale’s message becomes a reminder that life moves with a rhythm that isn’t always visible to us, and the moment we stop forcing outcomes, something softer and more meaningful begins to unfold. When you trust the timing of your life, you stop gripping so tightly to how things should happen and instead allow space for things to happen as they are meant to. Lucy describes how releasing control brings a kind of relief, because you realise you’re still steering your ship, but you don’t have to push against the current. When you’re aligned, doing your best, and showing up with intention, the next step becomes learning to let life meet you halfway. That means stepping into the things that scare you, becoming more visible, and letting go of the weight of other people’s opinions. There’s freedom in choosing to act without overthinking the reaction, freedom in trusting that the timing of your growth is perfect even when it’s unpredictable. Lucy’s reflection encourages you to surrender a little, believe in the quiet unfolding of your journey, and embrace every moment as part of something bigger working in your favour.

Speaker: @lucyhale

caption.text:
Comment “Banaras” and l’ll DM the details! ♥️

It’s happening from 5th to 7th December and it’s going to be an ALL GIRLS TRIP TO BANARAS - 16 girls (first come, first served).
I’m honestly so excited to share my love for this city with you and to help you see Banaras the way I did. If you’ve been following me, you know how much this city means to me.
After so many requests, I’m finally taking a group of girls with me to my favourite city and we are going to explore it like a local. All I ask is come with an open heart and pack you shoes because we will be walking a LOT!

I can’t wait to see some of you join in, this one’s really close to my heart! 

(banaras group trip for girls)

#banaras #grouptrips

caption.text:
There’s a version of you in another timeline who has already solved the problems you’re facing today.

Quantum Jumping helps you meet that version and bring their clarity, confidence, and wisdom into this reality.

@vishen will be exploring this practice at the Mindvalley Spiritual Summit 2025.

Comment below “Soul” to experience this live.

caption.text:
There’s a difference between the act of sex and lustful thoughts.

The sexual act is natural, but being lustful means you’re obsessively carrying sex in your mind all the time.

This can take away your focus from everything in life, bring down your energy considerably and make your personality unpleasant and dull.

Lust is a result of Svadhishthana Chakra imbalance. The two main complementary energies there are lust and creativity. So pick up something creative and you’ll see your energy change.

Practice the Kamajayi Mudra for 15mins a day to help get out of lust.

Jogging will instantly clear your mind of everything. A cold shower will instantly shift your thoughts and pull up your energies.
And volunteer work is another great way to shift your focus to something bigger.

Try any or all of these and you’ll definitely see a difference

#lust #sex #chakras #energy #yoga #lifestyle #health #wellness

caption.text:
Starting a new lil series called Gali Gali Dilli 💛
Because Dilli sirf India Gate ya CP nahi hoti...

Gali Gali Delhi – Episode 1

Found a hidden pocket of peace in Mehrauli —
Ashoka Mission is a Buddhist temple that looks straight out of a pahadi town ⛰️🌿

📍Location: Ashoka Mission, Mehrauli
🚈Nearest Metro station: Saket/Malviya nagar(yellow line)
⏱️ Timing: Usually open from morning till sunset
🙏 It’s a temple, so dress modestly and keep it respectful.
-No entry charges 
-No parking charges 

About be aware of dogs: 

I went there early in the morning, not knowing that there would be a group of dogs right near the entry gate barking super aggressively.
I honestly got really scared. I couldn’t even run because I was sure they’d chase me.

I just stood still until two locals who live nearby came and helped. They pushed the dogs away and walked me inside.
Later I spoke to the guard, and he mentioned that since very few people come here, the dogs bark at anyone they see.

So my advice: don’t go alone, especially if you're uncomfortable around dogs.
And for extra safety, maybe carry a stick or go with someone who’s familiar with the place.
Better to be safe than stuck like I was 😅

This is just the start, more episodes coming soon🍂

No repost

(Travel, delhi, ashoka mission, mehrauli, buddhist, explore, delhiblogger, hidden gems, south delhi, vacations, mountains)

caption.text:
I kid you not, this works.

Even though I try to eat clean, there is still a lot of inflammation that happens due to many lifestyle choices specially around this time of the year; so I started having this age-old Ayurvedic drink about a month back and in less than 7 days, I could fully feel it was working for me. 
My new acne subsided really fast and skin was clearer than it had been in a few months; my stomach was completely debloated and of course it is all about the gut which seemed to have got a much-needed deep cleanse.
Here’s what I do:
Soak 1 tsp each of ajwain, cumin and fennel seeds in a glass of water overnight.
Warm it on stove in the morning (don’t boil), and strain it.
Add a pinch of pink salt & few drops of lemon and sip it first thing in the morning

This is going to detoxify & cleanse your gut so fast and so well; you totally need to add this to your morning routine specially now that festive binging is about to happen.

If there’s any morning drink you swear by, share below!
.
.
.
.
.
.
